keys.description: REQUIRED. Shared api.data.gov key used by both api.congress.gov and FEC OpenFEC. One key covers both. Free, instant signup at https://api.data.gov/signup/.
keys.notes: One key powers api.congress.gov-backed sources and FEC OpenFEC. Senate roll-call ingest runs through a separate zero-key source (voteview.com).

keys.description: OPTIONAL UPGRADE. Reps-by-address via API. Default path uses the zero-key local shapefile pipeline; Geocodio trades disk footprint for API simplicity. Free tier 2500 lookups/day.
keys.notes: Used ahead of the local shapefile resolver when a key is configured.

keys.description: OPTIONAL UPGRADE. State bills and votes with individual member positions. Without a key, state bill lookup is narrative-only via LLM search; state vote positions and state change-detection require this.
keys.notes: State legislative coverage is out of scope for the wired runtime today.

keys.description: OPTIONAL UPGRADE. State bills fallback or primary source. Free tier 30,000 queries/month. Covers federal and state in one key if the user prefers unified access.
keys.notes: State legislative coverage is out of scope for the wired runtime today.

keys.description: OPTIONAL UPGRADE. Federal campaign-finance derived analytics such as industry rollups and revolving-door context. Non-commercial use only. Without a key, narrative context falls back to LLM search, but never for dollar amounts.
keys.notes: The candidate research tool intentionally stops at FEC totals today.

keys.description: OPTIONAL UPGRADE. State-level campaign finance. Without this, state finance data is explicitly not covered.
keys.notes: No state-finance adapter currently reads this key.

keys.description: OPTIONAL UPGRADE. Candidate bios for ballot explanations. Default bios come from LLM search and are tagged as low-confidence narrative; Vote Smart provides structured, verified bios.
keys.notes: The runtime still routes candidate-bio enrichment through the guarded web-search layer.

keys.description: OPTIONAL UPGRADE. Ballot logistics such as dates, deadlines, and polling places. Partner-gated; requires an application. Default uses Google Civic voterInfoQuery (free, less reliable).
keys.notes: Ballot logistics currently come from state adapters or Google Civic.

keys.description: OPTIONAL UPGRADE (paid). Local municipal, county, and school-board representative coverage. This is the only local source; without it, local reps are explicitly not covered.
keys.notes: Municipal, county, and state-legislative representative coverage is not implemented today.

keys.description: OPTIONAL UPGRADE (commercial). Structured down-ballot data. Default scope covers federal, statewide, and six secretary-of-state feeds; BallotReady lights up fuller down-ballot coverage.
keys.notes: Curated down-ballot contest enrichment beyond Google Civic is not wired.

keys.description: OPTIONAL but required for politiclaw_get_my_ballot. Google Cloud API key with the Civic Information API enabled for voterInfoQuery. Distinct from api.data.gov; create it in the Google Cloud console.
keys.notes: Required for every ballot tool. Per-state SoS adapters were scoped out in v1 after an audit found none of the six candidate states publishes a public address-to-ballot JSON feed; revisit when BallotReady or Democracy Works provides self-serve keys. Judicial retention detail and ballot-measure plain-language enrichment are not wired.

apiKeys.description: One key required (apiDataGov); all others are optional upgrades. The required key powers federal bills, House roll-call votes, committee schedules, and FEC finance data. Senate roll-call votes ingest separately through voteview.com (zero-key, no signup).

apiDataGov.description: REQUIRED. Shared api.data.gov key used by both api.congress.gov and FEC OpenFEC. One key covers both. Free, instant signup at https://api.data.gov/signup/.

geocodio.description: OPTIONAL UPGRADE. Reps-by-address via API. Default path uses the zero-key local shapefile pipeline; Geocodio trades disk footprint for API simplicity. Free tier 2500 lookups/day.

openStates.description: OPTIONAL UPGRADE. State bills and votes with individual member positions. Without a key, state bill lookup is narrative-only via LLM search; state vote positions and state change-detection require this.

legiscan.description: OPTIONAL UPGRADE. State bills fallback or primary source. Free tier 30,000 queries/month. Covers federal and state in one key if the user prefers unified access.

openSecrets.description: OPTIONAL UPGRADE. Federal campaign-finance derived analytics such as industry rollups and revolving-door context. Non-commercial use only. Without a key, narrative context falls back to LLM search, but never for dollar amounts.

followTheMoney.description: OPTIONAL UPGRADE. State-level campaign finance. Without this, state finance data is explicitly not covered.

voteSmart.description: OPTIONAL UPGRADE. Candidate bios for ballot explanations. Default bios come from LLM search and are tagged as low-confidence narrative; Vote Smart provides structured, verified bios.

democracyWorks.description: OPTIONAL UPGRADE. Ballot logistics such as dates, deadlines, and polling places. Partner-gated; requires an application. Default uses Google Civic voterInfoQuery (free, less reliable).

cicero.description: OPTIONAL UPGRADE (paid). Local municipal, county, and school-board representative coverage. This is the only local source; without it, local reps are explicitly not covered.

ballotReady.description: OPTIONAL UPGRADE (commercial). Structured down-ballot data. Default scope covers federal, statewide, and six secretary-of-state feeds; BallotReady lights up fuller down-ballot coverage.

googleCivic.description: OPTIONAL but required for politiclaw_get_my_ballot. Google Cloud API key with the Civic Information API enabled for voterInfoQuery. Distinct from api.data.gov; create it in the Google Cloud console.

sources.description: Per-source configuration. Keys here are independent of apiKeys; they control adapter selection and base URLs for self-hosted mirrors.

scraperBaseUrl.description: OPTIONAL. Base URL for a self-hosted unitedstates/congress scraper output mirror. When set, bill lookups fall back to this mirror if apiDataGov is absent or rate-limited. Adapter expects the canonical layout: {baseUrl}/{congress}/bills/{billtype}/{billtype}{number}/data.json. No default — the scraper is designed to be self-hosted and we do not endorse any third-party mirror.
